behaviorsAudited.behavior: The toolJSX state value is passed verbatim into <Messages toolJSX={toolJSX}> so the upstream-mirrored animation gate at runtime/src/agenc/upstream/components/Messages.tsx:595 (canAnimate = (!toolJSX || !!toolJSX.shouldContinueAnimation) && !toolUseConfirmQueue.length && !isMessageSelectorVisible) actually receives the live toolJSX value (R2 wiring is the load-bearing change; this row asserts the gate is reachable)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:B3.1 App.tsx passes the live toolJSX state variable to <Messages>, not a literal null or normalized copy

behaviorsAudited.behavior: Permission overlay rendering condition is unchanged (renders when permissionRequests[0] is truthy) - upstream parity here is 'dialog still renders when toolJSX is set' which AgenC already does, NOT a new gate
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/openclaude/App.tsx:279-282 (sibling JSX, not nested inside the toolJSX !== null branch); permission-bridge.tsx:118-121 (overlay only checks `request`, never `toolJSX`)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:B3.2 App.tsx renders OpenClaudePermissionOverlay unconditionally on permissionRequests[0] (dialog still mounts when toolJSX is set)

behaviorsAudited.behavior: isMessageSelectorVisible passed to <Messages> is false (parity with AgenC's no-message-selector host) so the Messages animation gate's third clause never blocks animation due to a missing AgenC feature
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:B3.3 App.tsx passes isMessageSelectorVisible={false} to <Messages> so the third canAnimate clause never spuriously blocks animation

behaviorsAudited.behavior: toolUseConfirmQueue array passed to <Messages> reflects the live confirm queue from buildToolUseConfirmQueue(permissionRequests, tools) so the animation gate's second clause respects pending confirmations
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:B3.4 App.tsx passes the live buildToolUseConfirmQueue result to <Messages> so the queue-blocks clause respects pending confirmations

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Building a Messages.tsx canAnimate computation with toolJSX=null AND empty toolUseConfirmQueue AND isMessageSelectorVisible=false yields canAnimate=true (REPL.tsx:595)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:E3.1 toolJSX=null AND empty toolUseConfirmQueue AND !isMessageSelectorVisible -> canAnimate=true

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Computation with toolJSX={shouldContinueAnimation:true} AND empty queue yields canAnimate=true (set-but-continuing path)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:E3.2 toolJSX={shouldContinueAnimation:true} AND empty queue -> canAnimate=true (set-but-continuing path)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Computation with toolJSX={shouldContinueAnimation:undefined, jsx:'X', shouldHidePromptInput:false} AND empty queue yields canAnimate=false (set-and-blocking path)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:E3.3 toolJSX set without shouldContinueAnimation -> canAnimate=false (set-and-blocking path)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Computation with toolJSX=null AND non-empty toolUseConfirmQueue yields canAnimate=false (queue-blocks path)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:E3.4 toolJSX=null AND non-empty toolUseConfirmQueue -> canAnimate=false (queue-blocks path)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Computation with toolJSX=null AND empty queue AND isMessageSelectorVisible=true yields canAnimate=false (selector-blocks path)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:E3.5 toolJSX=null AND empty queue AND isMessageSelectorVisible=true -> canAnimate=false (selector-blocks path)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: App.tsx source passes the toolJSX state variable, an empty/derived toolUseConfirmQueue, and isMessageSelectorVisible={false} to <Messages> (asserted by reading App.tsx)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx:B3.1 + B3.3 + B3.4 collectively cover the three prop assertions

approvedDivergenceReview.rationale: Verified the approved divergence is consistent with the current AgenC host architecture: a `grep` for SpinnerWithVerb / showSpinner / idleTimer / idleNotification in runtime/src/tui/openclaude/App.tsx returns zero matches, confirming the AgenC host renders neither the upstream `<SpinnerWithVerb>` (REPL.tsx:4653) nor the upstream idle-notification timer (REPL.tsx:4005), and does not implement the upstream `showSpinner` gate (REPL.tsx:1704). PromptInput owns its own spinner-adjacent UI internally. Adding host-level streaming-status surfaces would require new state plumbing (apiMetricsRef, responseLengthRef, streamMode, hasRunningTeammates, pendingWorkerRequest, command-queue length) that does not exist in AgenC today and is outside the streaming-tool-use parity scope. The toolJSX-driven animation gate is correctly preserved through the upstream-mirrored Messages.tsx:595, which IS the load-bearing parity surface for this row, and the permission overlay still mounts on permissionRequests[0] independent of toolJSX (mirroring REPL.tsx:2061-2062 'show unless blocked by toolJSX'). Deferring the host spinner / idle timer to a separate future contract is the correct scoping choice.

summary: R3 implementation correctly wires the live toolJSX state variable, the live buildToolUseConfirmQueue(permissionRequests, tools) result, and isMessageSelectorVisible={false} into <Messages> at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/App.tsx:258-273, ensuring the upstream-mirrored canAnimate gate at runtime/src/agenc/upstream/components/Messages.tsx:595 receives all three of its inputs verbatim. The OpenClaudePermissionOverlay is rendered at sibling-JSX level (App.tsx:279), gated only on permissionRequests[0] and never wrapped in any toolJSX-derived condition, matching upstream REPL.tsx:2061-2062 'permission/interactive dialogs show unless blocked by toolJSX' (and AgenC has no toolJSX-blocking case for this overlay because the divergence approves dropping host-level dialog focus arbitration). The five canAnimate truth-table tests (E3.1 through E3.5) cover all four logical input combinations that produce true/false outcomes (null/null/false=true, set+continue/null/false=true, set+blocking/null/false=false, null/queue/false=false, null/null/selector=false) and exhaustively exercise each gate clause. The four source-regex tests (B3.1 through B3.4) lock in the exact prop wiring against literal-null regressions, accidental wrappings of the permission overlay in toolJSX gates, missing isMessageSelectorVisible={false}, and a missing buildToolUseConfirmQueue derivation. The approvedDivergence (no host SpinnerWithVerb or idle timer) is verifiable from App.tsx source and is correctly limited to host-level streaming-status surfaces that are outside the streaming-tool-use parity scope. All 9 tests in App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx pass.
